statement: The system shall provide a front‑facing camera that looks straight down to give an overview of the user's hands, and a foldable camera arm beneath the boom that supplies a three‑dimensional view displayed on a two‑dimensional screen.
The system shall respond to an emergency stop button press within one tenth of a second.
The system shall allow predefined arm movements to be stored in files and enable the boom to be moved to a predetermined position by pressing a button on the computer.
The system shall provide assistance during a tricky manoeuvre, such as placing an object between a series of tiles.
The system shall provide a Bolt Runner constraint feature that allows tightening a bolt regardless of orientation.
The system shall detect a delay of at least one millisecond and generate a notification indicating that operation should be paused for one minute.
The system shall break pre-programmed moves into stages, such as 20-degree increments, to allow the operator to monitor progress.
The system shall support high refresh rates even when operating at lower resolutions.
The system shall provide a ghost mode that indicates where the machine is moving.
The system shall allow users to preprogram virtual walls that block movement into restricted areas.
The system shall include a camera with an integrated light to create shadows for 3D spatial perception.
The system shall allow setting force ratios on the arms to restrict heavy loads to be picked up only in specified positions.
